seoTitle: How to Increase DPI in Canva for Print
metaDescription: Learn how to adjust DPI settings in Canva so your designs print sharp and professional instead of blurry.
intro: Low DPI is one of the most common reasons a printed design comes out blurry. This tutorial explains what DPI means for your Canva designs and walks through adjusting settings so your files are ready for high-quality printing.

steps.heading: Understand DPI and its importance
steps.body: Learn why dots per inch matters for print quality, especially compared to designs meant only for screens.

steps.heading: Adjust dimensions for higher resolution
steps.body: Set your design's dimensions and export settings to support a higher DPI output.

steps.heading: Select the right export settings
steps.body: Choose export options in Canva that prioritize print quality rather than a smaller, web-optimized file.

steps.heading: Apply this to print projects
steps.body: Use higher DPI settings for materials like flyers, business cards, or gallery prints that need to look sharp on paper.

faq.question: What DPI is generally recommended for print?
faq.answer: 300 DPI is a commonly used standard for high-quality print materials, though requirements can vary by print provider.

faq.question: Does increasing DPI make my file size larger?
faq.answer: Yes, higher DPI settings typically result in a larger file size due to the increased image detail.

faq.question: Can I increase DPI on any Canva design?
faq.answer: You can adjust export settings on most designs, though results depend on the resolution of any images already used.

faq.question: Is DPI important for designs that will only be viewed on screens?
faq.answer: Not really — DPI matters most for print, while screen designs are generally measured in pixel dimensions instead.
